title: Code Profile Manager editor config
description: Declarative desired state for one VS Code OSS-based editor's profiles. Layers [global] settings/extensions, reusable [groups.*], the built-in [default] profile, and named [profiles.*] into an effective state per profile. See docs/config.md for the full reference.

editor.description: How this config refers to / overrides the target editor.

global.description: Settings and extensions applied to every managed profile.

groups.description: Reusable bundles of settings and extensions that a profile can include by name.

default.description: The built-in Default profile (always present; cannot be renamed). Has no icon and no use_default flags.

profiles.description: Named (non-default) profiles, keyed by profile name. The Default profile is configured under [default], never here.

propertyNames.$comment: [profiles.Default] is rejected on load; use [default] instead.

managed.description: Managed/overlay mode. When true, push/pull manage only the profiles defined in this config (plus delete = true tombstones) and never create or delete undefined profiles. Default false = full mirror (the config owns the entire profile set).

extensionId.description: Extension identifier 'publisher.name', optionally pinned as 'publisher.name@version'. Pins are parsed but not enforced for membership.

settings.description: VS Code settings keys mapped to any JSON value. Keys are dotted setting names (e.g. "editor.formatOnSave"). null values are not supported (TOML has no null) and are stripped on write.

useDefault.description: Resource types this profile inherits from the Default profile (VS Code's useDefaultFlags). A true value means the editor reads the Default profile's copy and no profile-local file is written.

name.description: Editor name to match during discovery (product.json nameShort or applicationName), e.g. "VSCodium" or "Code - OSS".

binary.description: Explicit launcher path, bypassing PATH discovery.

user_dir.description: Override for the editor's User/ directory (wrapper script, --user-data-dir, or portable install).

extensions_dir.description: Override for the shared extensions directory. Note: Code - OSS and VSCodium share .vscode-oss by default.

settings.description: Settings keys applied to profiles using this layer.

extensions.description: Extension IDs applied to profiles using this layer.

groups.description: Names of groups this profile includes.

exclude_extensions.description: Extension IDs to drop even if a group or global adds them.

icon.description: Codicon ID used as the profile icon (e.g. "package", "briefcase"). See the codicon reference: https://microsoft.github.io/vscode-codicons/dist/codicon.html

groups.description: Names of groups this profile includes (applied in listed order).

exclude_extensions.description: Extension IDs to drop even if a group or global adds them.

use_default.description: Resource types this profile inherits from the Default profile.

delete.description: Tombstone: delete this profile from the editor if it exists. Valid only in overlay mode ([options] managed = true or --profile). Must not be combined with any other field.

then.description: A tombstone (delete = true) must carry no other fields.
